sources_audited.note: The Census archive supports the stated 541820 scope and explicitly includes lobbying, political consulting, and PR consulting, but it does not measure the 0.62 eligible-firm share; that value is honestly labeled ESTIMATE.

BLS supports the cited duties, 2024 employment, 2024-2034 growth from 315,900 to 331,000, demand rationale, and 13% broader-industry employment share. It does not directly measure LMM-agency task exposure, implementation, outsourced demand, or operator dependence, as the packet discloses.

Axios supports the 88% efficiency expectation, listed AI-supported PR tasks, time-based billing exposure, 1,685 billed hours in 2022, and in-house substitution risk. It does not establish realized labor removal or the numeric a, rho, q, or o values, which remain ESTIMATE.

The ANA/4As announcement supports approximately seven-year average tenure, relatively long PR-agency relationships, and majority AOR/retainer status among agencies' top-ten clients. It does not show that AI-created savings are retained at renewal, a limitation the packet states.

Gallup supports the 52.3% age statistic and the fall-2024 survey result that 22% of employer-business owners planned a sale, transfer, or public offering within five years. The population is neither PR-specific nor LMM-sized and intentions are not completed qualifying control transfers; the PROXY bridge discloses these transformations.

PRovoke supports 80 identified PR-sector deals in 2025 versus 99 in 2024, a larger-seller tilt, and active independent-agency buyers. Its coverage and denominator do not identify a U.S. LMM five-year transfer probability, as disclosed.

Ignition supports a 2025 survey of 273 agency managers and executives and the stated 28% hourly, 28% productized/subscription, 10% retainer, and 25% project pricing shares. The sample spans several agency categories and does not isolate PR, as disclosed.

caveats: Every researched base primitive is an ESTIMATE or PROXY, and the injected firm-count anchor n is also ESTIMATE; the PRIORITY base is therefore model-conditioned rather than directly observed.

The base tier is sensitive to the broad national-owner transfer proxy: s5 at its registered low reduces F enough for the all-base/low-s5 case to fall below the PRIORITY breadth cut. This is uncertainty already represented by the LOW_PRIORITY-to-HIGHEST_PRIORITY envelope, not a source defect.
The demand bridge uses a national occupation dominated by employers outside advertising, PR, and related services; it is directionally useful but cannot isolate outsourced agency demand.
Pricing and relationship sources span broader agency populations and do not directly measure how much AI productivity an LMM PR agency retains after repricing and renewal.

The disclosed recurring-service lens coherently excludes election-cycle and isolated one-off work, but a whole-code lens would likely produce different eligibility, transferability, pricing, and demand primitives.
summary: Mechanics pass. All seven score-driving sources are reachable and accurately represented at the factual level. The packet is candid that none directly measures the decisive LMM PR-agency primitives, uses explicit PROXY bridges for transferability and demand, keeps the remaining judgments as ESTIMATE, and publishes wide ranges spanning LOW_PRIORITY through HIGHEST_PRIORITY. The lens narrowing is disclosed and coherent, factor semantics are not reversed or double counted, and the narrative follows the registered evidence. The record is useful for validation-stage screening with caveats, and no source or construct defect meets the literal base-tier or action-category materiality test.
